map.condition: Recurrence of form T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n)
Comparing two functions for big-O order
Solving divide-and-conquer recurrence not fitting Master Theorem
Determining tight bound when upper and lower bounds match

formulas.name: Big-O (Upper Bound)
formulas.latex: f(n)=O(g(n)) \iff \exists\, c>0,\, n_0 \text{ s.t. } f(n)\le c\cdot g(n)\;\forall n\ge n_0
formulas.plain: f(n)=O(g(n)) iff there exist c>0 and n0 such that f(n) <= c*g(n) for all n >= n0
formulas.whenToUse: Proving a worst-case upper bound on runtime or space

terms.means: threshold beyond which the bound holds

formulas.trap: O( ) gives an upper bound, NOT an exact bound — O(n^3) is technically valid for O(n) algorithms

formulas.name: Big-Omega (Lower Bound)
formulas.latex: f(n)=\Omega(g(n)) \iff \exists\, c>0,\, n_0 \text{ s.t. } f(n)\ge c\cdot g(n)\;\forall n\ge n_0
formulas.plain: f(n)=Omega(g(n)) iff there exist c>0 and n0 such that f(n) >= c*g(n) for all n >= n0
formulas.whenToUse: Stating a lower bound (best-case or problem hardness lower bound)

terms.means: threshold beyond which the bound holds

formulas.trap: Omega is NOT the best case — it is a lower bound on growth rate independent of case

formulas.name: Big-Theta (Tight Bound)
formulas.latex: f(n)=\Theta(g(n)) \iff f(n)=O(g(n)) \text{ and } f(n)=\Omega(g(n))
formulas.plain: f(n)=Theta(g(n)) iff f(n)=O(g(n)) AND f(n)=Omega(g(n))
formulas.whenToUse: When exact asymptotic growth is known (upper and lower bounds are the same)
formulas.trap: Theta requires BOTH O and Omega; a function that is O(n log n) but Omega(n) is NOT Theta(n log n)

formulas.name: Little-o (Strict Upper Bound)
formulas.latex: f(n)=o(g(n)) \iff \lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{f(n)}{g(n)}=0
formulas.plain: f(n)=o(g(n)) iff lim_{n->inf} f(n)/g(n) = 0
formulas.whenToUse: When f grows strictly slower than g (f is dominated by g)
formulas.trap: f(n)=o(g(n)) implies f(n)=O(g(n)) but NOT vice versa

formulas.name: Limit-Based Comparison Test
formulas.latex: L=\lim_{n\to\infty}\frac{f(n)}{g(n)}\Rightarrow\begin{cases}L=0 & f=o(g)\\0<L<\infty & f=\Theta(g)\\L=\infty & f=\omega(g)\end{cases}
formulas.plain: L = lim f/g: L=0 => f=o(g), 0<L<inf => f=Theta(g), L=inf => f=omega(g)
formulas.whenToUse: Comparing any two functions to determine their asymptotic relationship

terms.means: limit of f(n)/g(n) as n tends to infinity

formulas.trap: The limit may not exist (oscillating functions); in such cases use the definition directly

formulas.name: Standard Growth Rate Ordering
formulas.latex: 1 \ll \log\log n \ll \log n \ll n^{\epsilon} \ll n \ll n\log n \ll n^2 \ll n^c \ll 2^n \ll n! \ll n^n
formulas.plain: 1 << log(log n) << log n << n^eps << n << n*log n << n^2 << n^c << 2^n << n! << n^n
formulas.whenToUse: Ranking functions by asymptotic growth; identifying the dominant term
formulas.trap: log n grows slower than ANY positive power of n, no matter how small the exponent

formulas.name: Logarithm Identities for Complexity
formulas.latex: \log(n^k)=k\log n;\quad \log_a n=\frac{\log_b n}{\log_b a};\quad n^{\log n}=2^{(\log n)^2}
formulas.plain: log(n^k) = k*log(n);  log_a(n) = log_b(n)/log_b(a);  n^log(n) = 2^((log n)^2)
formulas.whenToUse: Simplifying complex expressions involving logarithms before comparing growth rates
formulas.trap: 2^(3 log_2 n) = n^3, NOT 6n — apply log rules carefully before concluding O-class

formulas.name: Polynomial vs Exponential vs Factorial
formulas.latex: n^k = O(2^n)\text{ for all } k;\quad 2^n = O(n!)\quad\text{(Stirling: }n!\approx\sqrt{2\pi n}\left(\tfrac{n}{e}\right)^n\!)
formulas.plain: n^k = O(2^n) for all k;  2^n = O(n!);  n! ~ sqrt(2*pi*n) * (n/e)^n
formulas.whenToUse: Comparing algorithmic complexity classes (polynomial vs exponential vs factorial)
formulas.trap: c^n (c>1) is exponential regardless of base; log base only changes by a constant factor

formulas.name: Master Theorem — Three Cases
formulas.latex: T(n)=aT\!\left(\tfrac{n}{b}\right)+f(n),\;a\ge1,b>1\Rightarrow\begin{cases}\Theta(n^{\log_b a}) & f=O(n^{\log_b a-\epsilon})\\\Theta(n^{\log_b a}\log n) & f=\Theta(n^{\log_b a})\\\Theta(f(n)) & f=\Omega(n^{\log_b a+\epsilon}),\;af(n/b)\le cf(n)\end{cases}
formulas.plain: T(n)=a*T(n/b)+f(n): Case1: T=Theta(n^log_b(a)) if f=O(n^(log_b(a)-eps)); Case2: T=Theta(n^log_b(a)*logn) if f=Theta(n^log_b(a)); Case3: T=Theta(f(n)) if f=Omega(n^(log_b(a)+eps)) and regularity holds
formulas.whenToUse: Solving any divide-and-conquer recurrence that splits into a equal subproblems of size n/b

terms.means: factor by which input size is reduced
cost of divide and combine step

formulas.trap: Master Theorem does NOT apply when subproblems have different sizes (e.g. T(n)=T(n/3)+T(2n/3)+n)

formulas.name: Master Theorem Case 2 Extended (log^k factor)
formulas.latex: f(n)=\Theta(n^{\log_b a}\cdot\log^k n)\Rightarrow T(n)=\Theta(n^{\log_b a}\cdot\log^{k+1} n)
formulas.plain: If f(n)=Theta(n^log_b(a) * log^k(n)) then T(n)=Theta(n^log_b(a) * log^(k+1)(n))
formulas.whenToUse: When the combine step has an extra log factor (e.g. merge sort on linked lists)
formulas.trap: Forgetting to add 1 to the log exponent in Case 2 extended form

formulas.name: Common Recurrence Solutions (Reference Table)
formulas.latex: \begin{aligned}T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(n)&\Rightarrow O(n\log n)\\T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(1)&\Rightarrow O(n)\\T(n)=T(n/2)+O(1)&\Rightarrow O(\log n)\\T(n)=T(n/2)+O(n)&\Rightarrow O(n)\\T(n)=T(n-1)+O(n)&\Rightarrow O(n^2)\\T(n)=2T(n-1)+O(1)&\Rightarrow O(2^n)\end{aligned}
formulas.whenToUse: Quick lookup for standard algorithm recurrences in GATE MCQs

terms.means: time complexity as a function of input size n

formulas.trap: T(n)=T(n-1)+O(n) gives O(n^2), not O(n log n) — it telescopes to n+(n-1)+...+1

formulas.name: Telescoping / Back-Substitution
formulas.latex: T(n)=T(n-1)+f(n)\Rightarrow T(n)=T(1)+\sum_{k=2}^{n}f(k)
formulas.whenToUse: Linear recurrences where the index decreases by a constant each step

terms.means: cost at level k of the recursion

formulas.trap: Forgetting the summation bounds — always substitute until base case T(1) or T(0)

formulas.name: Recursion Tree Method
formulas.latex: T(n)=aT(n/b)+f(n)\Rightarrow \text{Total cost}=\sum_{i=0}^{\log_b n}a^i\cdot f\!\left(\tfrac{n}{b^i}\right)+\Theta(n^{\log_b a})
formulas.plain: Total cost = sum_{i=0..log_b(n)} a^i * f(n/b^i) + Theta(n^log_b(a))
formulas.whenToUse: When Master Theorem does not apply or needs verification (unequal splits, non-polynomial f(n))

terms.means: number of nodes at level i
cost per node at level i

formulas.trap: When splits are unequal (e.g. T(n/3)+T(2n/3)), the tree is NOT balanced — use the longest path for height

formulas.name: Substitution (Guess and Verify) Method
formulas.latex: \text{Guess } T(n)=O(g(n))\text{, then prove: } T(n)\le c\cdot g(n)-d\cdot h(n)
formulas.plain: Guess T(n)=O(g(n)), substitute into recurrence and prove T(n) <= c*g(n) for large n
formulas.whenToUse: Verifying a guessed closed-form solution using mathematical induction

terms.means: positive constants chosen to make induction work
slack term needed to absorb lower-order terms

formulas.trap: Proving T(n)<=cn*log(n) for T(n)=2T(n/2)+n: guess must subtract a lower-order term to close the induction

formulas.name: Akra-Bazzi Method (Generalized Master Theorem)
formulas.latex: T(n)=\sum_{i=1}^{k}a_i T(n/b_i)+f(n)\Rightarrow T(n)=\Theta\!\left(n^p\!\left(1+\int_1^n\frac{f(u)}{u^{p+1}}du\right)\right)
formulas.plain: T(n)=sum a_i*T(n/b_i)+f(n) where p satisfies sum a_i/b_i^p=1; T(n)=Theta(n^p*(1+integral))
formulas.whenToUse: Divide-and-conquer recurrences with unequal split sizes (e.g. T(n)=T(n/3)+T(2n/3)+n)

terms.means: number of subproblems and their size factors

formulas.trap: Standard Master Theorem fails for unequal splits; must use Akra-Bazzi or recursion tree

formulas.name: Sorting Algorithm Complexities
formulas.latex: \begin{aligned}\text{Merge Sort}&: O(n\log n)\\\text{Quick Sort}&: O(n\log n)\text{ avg}, O(n^2)\text{ worst}\\\text{Heap Sort}&: O(n\log n)\\\text{Insertion Sort}&: O(n^2)\\\text{Counting Sort}&: O(n+k)\end{aligned}
formulas.plain: Merge Sort: O(n log n); Quick Sort: O(n log n) avg / O(n^2) worst; Heap Sort: O(n log n); Insertion Sort: O(n^2); Counting Sort: O(n+k)
formulas.whenToUse: Comparing sorting algorithms in algorithm design / time complexity questions

terms.means: range of input values (for counting sort)

formulas.trap: Quick sort has O(n^2) worst case (already sorted input with naive pivot) — the average is O(n log n)

formulas.name: Graph Algorithm Complexities
formulas.latex: \begin{aligned}\text{BFS / DFS}&: O(V+E)\\\text{Dijkstra (min-heap)}&: O((V+E)\log V)\\\text{Bellman-Ford}&: O(VE)\\\text{Floyd-Warshall}&: O(V^3)\\\text{Prim (binary heap)}&: O((V+E)\log V)\\\text{Kruskal}&: O(E\log E)\end{aligned}
formulas.plain: BFS/DFS: O(V+E); Dijkstra (min-heap): O((V+E) log V); Bellman-Ford: O(VE); Floyd-Warshall: O(V^3); Prim: O((V+E) log V); Kruskal: O(E log E)
formulas.whenToUse: Algorithm design questions involving graphs, shortest paths, spanning trees
formulas.trap: BFS/DFS are O(V+E), NOT O(V*E) — adjacency list representation is assumed

formulas.name: Search & Basic Operation Complexities
formulas.latex: \begin{aligned}\text{Binary Search}&: O(\log n)\\\text{BST (balanced)}&: O(\log n)\\\text{BST (unbalanced)}&: O(n)\\\text{Hash Table (avg)}&: O(1)\\\text{Heap insert/delete}&: O(\log n)\\\text{Build Heap}&: O(n)\end{aligned}
formulas.plain: Binary Search: O(log n); Balanced BST: O(log n); Unbalanced BST: O(n); Hash Table avg: O(1); Heap insert/delete: O(log n); Build Heap: O(n)
formulas.whenToUse: Questions about data structure operation costs or selecting appropriate structures

terms.means: number of elements in the structure

formulas.trap: Building a heap by inserting one-by-one is O(n log n); the Floyd heapify method is O(n)

formulas.name: Space Complexity Key Rules
formulas.latex: \text{Space}=\text{Auxiliary space}+\text{Input space};\quad\text{In-place}\Leftrightarrow O(1)\text{ aux}
formulas.plain: Total space = Auxiliary space + Input space; In-place algorithm uses O(1) auxiliary space
formulas.whenToUse: Evaluating whether an algorithm is in-place or computing total memory usage

terms.means: extra space used beyond input storage

formulas.trap: Merge sort needs O(n) auxiliary space so it is NOT in-place, even though it sorts efficiently

formulas.name: Recursive Call Stack Space
formulas.latex: S(n)=O(\text{recursion depth})\times O(\text{stack frame size})
formulas.plain: Space = O(recursion depth) * O(stack frame size); e.g. DFS on graph: O(V) stack space
formulas.whenToUse: Computing auxiliary space of recursive algorithms

terms.means: maximum number of simultaneous active calls
memory per function call (local vars, return address)

formulas.trap: Tail-recursive algorithms may use O(1) stack if optimized by compiler, but typically assumed O(depth)

formulas.name: Arithmetic Rules for Asymptotic Notation
formulas.latex: \begin{aligned}O(f)+O(g)&=O(\max(f,g))\\O(f)\cdot O(g)&=O(f\cdot g)\\c\cdot O(f)&=O(f)\text{ for constant }c\\O(\log^k n)&=O(n^\epsilon)\text{ for any }\epsilon>0\end{aligned}
formulas.plain: O(f)+O(g)=O(max(f,g)); O(f)*O(g)=O(f*g); c*O(f)=O(f); log^k(n)=O(n^eps) for any eps>0
formulas.whenToUse: Simplifying compound complexity expressions; identifying the dominant term
formulas.trap: O(f)+O(g)=O(f+g) NOT O(f*g); also 2^(2n) is NOT O(2^n) — constants in exponents matter

formulas.name: Transitivity and Reflexivity of Notations
formulas.latex: f=O(g),\;g=O(h)\Rightarrow f=O(h);\quad f=O(f)\;(\text{reflexive});\quad f=\Theta(g)\Rightarrow g=\Theta(f)
formulas.plain: Transitivity: f=O(g) and g=O(h) => f=O(h). Reflexivity: f=O(f). Theta is symmetric.
formulas.whenToUse: Chaining inequalities between functions; proving asymptotic relationships transitively
formulas.trap: O is NOT symmetric: f=O(g) does NOT imply g=O(f) (unless f=Theta(g))

formulas.name: Key Summation Formulas (for telescoping recurrences)
formulas.whenToUse: Evaluating total cost after telescoping linear or quadratic recurrences
formulas.trap: Harmonic sum sum(1/k, k=1..n) = Theta(log n), not O(1) — appears in average-case analysis of algorithms like QuickSort
